In uncertain moments, humans do what humans do best:
reach for control.
And sometimes, that sense of control comes in the form of an antibiotic.

We asked people what they think antibiotics are.
Honest answers. Familiar habits.
Then an expert stepped in, not just to clarify, but to break the false sense of safety we’ve built around them.
Because “feels right” isn’t always right.

Antibiotics aren’t comfort pills.
They’re precision tools.

One minute of truth for a lifetime of better choices.
 #AntibioticNahiAadatBadlo  #1MinuteForAMR #healthcare #genomics #Diagnostics

What looks like a simple experiment…
is actually evolution happening in real time.

This Harvard study showed how bacteria don’t just die under antibiotics, they adapt, survive, and grow stronger.

Antibiotics are essential.
But their misuse creates the perfect conditions for resistance.

Use them right. Use them when needed.

#AntibioticResistance #AMR #microbiology #PublicHealth #sciencecommunication

For years, the world saw extraordinary endurance.

What remained unseen was the genetics behind it.

Eero Mäntyranta carried a rare mutation that changed how his body delivered oxygen, influencing both his endurance and his health.

Rare conditions aren’t confined to textbooks or research papers.
They exist in people.

And understanding them is where better care begins.

#RareDiseaseAwareness #Genomics #PrecisionHealth #Identfi

On World TB Day, we stepped out to ask a simple question:
What do people really think is the bigger threat in India?

The answers reflected a common perception.
But the reality tells a different story.

While the world’s attention shifted rapidly during the pandemic, tuberculosis continued to impact lives across the country quietly and persistently.

At HaystackAnalytics, we believe addressing TB at scale requires faster and more advanced diagnostics. Our goal is to make NGS-based solutions accessible across the country, enabling better detection and stronger clinical decisions.

Because meaningful change happens when innovation reaches those who need it most. As we reflect on World TB Day, we continue working towards a TB Mukt Bharat.

In support of the national mission led by Hon’ble PM Narendra Modi and the continued efforts of NTEP India to eliminate tuberculosis, we remain committed to contributing towards the vision of a TB Mukt Bharat.

#WorldTBDay #EndTB #TBMuktBharat #NGS #Diagnostics

1 Week, 1 Rare Disorder — Congenital Hypothyroidism (CH)

Babies who sleep too much, feed too slowly, and stay too quiet… sometimes it’s not just “normal.”
It could be Congenital Hypothyroidism  where the thyroid gland doesn’t make enough hormones needed for growth and brain development. Often, it’s genetic — linked to genes like
𝘗𝘈𝘟8, 𝘛𝘚𝘏𝘙, 𝘋𝘜𝘖𝘟2.

With early diagnosis, babies can receive timely care and thrive.
That’s where DGx Pediatric Screening helps  by identifying genetic changes early in life.

See you next week as we decode another rare disorder  simply.

#raredisease #pediatrichealth #genetics #genomics #healthcare #PublicHealth

Not just rare — real and detectable.

This World Children’s Day, we begin a journey of awareness.

1 Week, 1 Rare Disorder
Episode 1: Beta Thalassemia

A genetic blood disorder caused by changes in the HBB gene –
common across India, South Asia, and the Mediterranean.
It affects thousands of lives every year…
many of which could have been protected with timely diagnosis.

DGx Pediatric Screening enables early detection and early intervention —
for proactive care and a healthier tomorrow.

World Children’s Day | DGx Pediatrics Screening 

🧬 Stay tuned as we decode one rare disorder every week.

Smarter decisions. Faster answers. Better outcomes for little lungs.

Dr. Parul Shukla, Pediatrician - shares how FLU-NGS is transforming the way pediatric respiratory infections are diagnosed and managed. With painless sampling, comprehensive detection, and results delivered in just 24 hours, it gives doctors the clarity they need to make the right treatment decisions — including when antibiotics are not necessary.

This not only improves clinical outcomes but also brings immense relief to parents, who gain answers without long waits or unnecessary medications. For children, it means faster recovery, gentler care, and a healthier future — all starting with a single, powerful test.

#PublicHealth #HealthCare #Diagnostic #Genomics #pediatriccare

This case reflects the growing need for precision diagnostics — and how infexn™ NGS delivers clarity when complexity rises.

When organs start shutting down, it isn’t just a complication — it’s Sepsis. A silent killer that can turn deadly within hours.
Routine tests often fail to keep up with its speed.

That’s where infexn™ NGS transforms the game — a single-test solution for infectious diseases with a rapid 24-hour turnaround time.

Because in Sepsis, timely diagnosis isn’t just important… it saves lives.

World Sepsis Day – Raising awareness, driving change.

A complex infection. Multiple tests. Yet, every result came back negative.
Sputum samples, upper respiratory panels, and broncho cultures all failed to provide clarity, while the patient’s condition continued to deteriorate.

Dr. Siddhant Shetty shares his experience with this challenging case, where conventional diagnostics fell short. Turning to sequencing with infexn™ NGS, he was able to identify the accurate pathogen — guiding the right treatment and changing the course of the patient’s care.

This case highlights the critical need for precision diagnostics and demonstrates how infexn™ NGS delivers clarity when conventional tests reach their limits.
